summary: Tested new editing capabilities (PUT department/division/user) for both backend and frontend. Backend: 44/44 pytest tests pass — 29 existing regression + 15 new editing tests covering role-scoped permissions (GM/DM/DivM/employee/self), denormalization of target_department_name in activities on dept rename, denormalization of employee_name on user rename, password-change → re-login success, and 400/403/404 error paths. Frontend (Expo web preview, 390x844): admin login OK; /departments edit pencil → modal edit-dept-input → save-edit-dept persists and updates list; /users edit pencil → edit-user-name + edit-user-password modal → save-user-edit persists; /divisions edit pencil → edit-div-input → save-edit-div persists. All edit modals open with correct testIDs and API calls succeed. No red screen. Only pre-existing deprecation warnings (shadow* style props, pointerEvents) — non-blocking.

react_native_issues.issue: React-native-web deprecation warning: shadow* props should migrate to boxShadow. Non-blocking, carried over from prior iteration.
Deprecation warning: props.pointerEvents is deprecated — use style.pointerEvents. Non-blocking.

action_items: (Optional) Migrate deprecated shadow* → boxShadow and props.pointerEvents → style.pointerEvents to silence RN-Web warnings.

Note: users.tsx save button testID is 'save-user-edit' (not 'save-edit-user' as mentioned in the request). Current tests use the actual testID present in code — no change needed unless naming convention should be aligned.
critical_code_review_comments: PUT /api/users/{id}: GM cannot edit employees or division_managers directly per current implementation (only department_managers + self). This is intentional per spec but worth double-checking with product requirements — request wording says 'GM can update department_manager names+passwords' which is exactly implemented.
server.py update_department uses payload.name.strip() but relies on Pydantic to require the field; empty-string case returns 400 correctly. Good.
update_user correctly denormalizes employee_name into db.activities on rename — validated via test.
seed_data_creation: Test data prefixed TEST_* (departments, divisions, users) created during backend + UI runs. Not cleaned; small volume, acceptable.
context_for_next_testing_agent: New testIDs added: edit-dept-{id}, edit-dept-input, save-edit-dept, edit-div-{id}, edit-div-input, save-edit-div, edit-user-{id}, edit-user-name, edit-user-password, save-user-edit. Backend PUT endpoints: /api/departments/{id}, /api/divisions/{id}, /api/users/{id}. Permission matrix: GM→dept (rename), dept (dept_mgr users), self; DM→own-dept divisions & users (divm+employee) & self; DivM→own-div employees & self; Employee→self only. Denormalization confirmed for target_department_name (on dept rename) and employee_name (on user rename).
rca_of_issue: N/A — all editing features working correctly, no bugs found.
